stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 5 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 15 observations with mean 57.4 points (within-school SD = 9.0). school B has 10 observations with mean 52.5 points (within-school SD = 9.9). school C has 5 observations with mean 56.1 points (within-school SD = 4.3). school D has 8 observations with mean 60.4 points (within-school SD = 8.6). school E has 10 observations with mean 47.4 points (within-school SD = 5.0). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.7 and 16.6 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school C: 56.1 points (shrunk from raw 56.1). Range [56.0, 56.1] as between-school SD varies from 6.7 to 16.6. Pooled mean: 54.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 54.7 points (weighted average of 5 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=11.1: B = 0.971.
Interval [56.0, 56.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (56.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (54.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
